headline: Starbucks (SBUX) Is Replacing Microsoft And IBM Software To Cut $400 Million Spend

headline: The 20-Year Dividend Strategy Built For Investors Who Don’t Need Income Yet
summary: An investor who bought Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) ten years ago paid closer to $50 per share than $45. headline: UK to regulate cloud service providers Microsoft, Google and others to protect financial stability
summary: Britain on Friday designated ‌cloud service providers Microsoft, ‌Google, Amazon and Oracle as 'critical ​third parties', bringing them under direct regulatory oversight to protect the country's financial ‌system. "As banks, ⁠insurers and financial market infrastructures become increasingly ⁠reliant on cloud services, disruption at a major ​supplier could ​affect ​multiple firms at ‌the same time, potentially impacting services customers depend on," the government said in a statement. To that end, ‌it designated Microsoft ​Ireland Operations ​Ltd, Google ​Cloud EMEA Ltd, ‌Amazon Web Services ​EMEA SARL, ​and Oracle Corporation UK Ltd as critical third ​parties, ‌effective July 13.

content.title: UK to regulate cloud service providers Microsoft, Google and others to protect financial stability
content.summary: Britain on Friday designated ‌cloud service providers Microsoft, ‌Google, Amazon and Oracle as 'critical ​third parties', bringing them under direct regulatory oversight to protect the country's financial ‌system. "As banks, ⁠insurers and financial market infrastructures become increasingly ⁠reliant on cloud services, disruption at a major ​supplier could ​affect ​multiple firms at ‌the same time, potentially impacting services customers depend on," the government said in a statement. To that end, ‌it designated Microsoft ​Ireland Operations ​Ltd, Google ​Cloud EMEA Ltd, ‌Amazon Web Services ​EMEA SARL, ​and Oracle Corporation UK Ltd as critical third ​parties, ‌effective July 13.

headline: Microsoft (MSFT) Stock Looks Cheap On Earnings While Cash Flow Drives Debate
summary: Microsoft stock has pulled back recently, yet valuation checks still point to a potential gap between its current price and an intrinsic value estimate built from a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model that screens the shares as undervalued. Microsoft has returned 42.5% over the past 5 years, which is a solid gain but not so extreme that valuation no longer matters for new money going into the stock. Heavy investment in AI, including the new Microsoft Frontier Company unit, can support long term...

headline: Top Wall Street Analyst Says AI Spending Is Delivering Real Returns: “When We Build a Data Center, It’s Already Pre-Sold”
summary: Hyperscalers are posting record capital expenditures while free cash flow craters, yet Microsoft, Amazon, and Alphabet insist every data center is already sold before the first server goes in. A top Wall Street analyst breaks down whether investors are misreading the returns or being misled about them.

headline: Another AI casualty: CVC investments in startups
summary: Featured image by Jenna O’Malley/Pitchbook CVCs are participating in a decade-low percentage of all venture deals as corporates grapple with how best to use their dollars in the new AI era. As corporate investors navigate ballooning AI bills, many are also focusing on larger and more impactful deals to transform their own businesses. Big-dollar investments into AI startups are getting done at the C-suite level, not through venture arms, said Nagraj Kashyap, general partner at VC firm Touring Cap
